sentence: Subjective:
HPI: Mr. Roberts is a 49-year-old male with a history of hypertension and palpitations, who was recently seen in the emergency room with high blood pressure and palpitations.
He attributes the episode to a panic attack, possibly triggered by consuming a few cups of coffee and not having breakfast.
He has a family history of hypertension and has been on a new medication, Benicar HCT (hydrochlorothiazide), for about a month, in addition to Amlodipine 10 mg daily.
He reports checking his blood pressure daily and has been seeing readings of 146/155.
He has been trying to reduce his salt intake and is doing some exercise by walking on a treadmill.
He denies any pain or discomfort during the physical examination.
Past medical history: He has hypertension and a family history of hypertension.
Review of systems: [NOT MENTIONED]
Current medications: Benicar HCT (hydrochlorothiazide) [DOSE NOT MENTIONED] [FREQUENCY NOT MENTIONED] [INDICATION NOT MENTIONED] Amlodipine 10 mg daily.
Objective:
Vital signs: Blood pressure 146/155 mmHg, pulse rate [NOT MENTIONED], oxygen saturation [NOT MENTIONED], temperature [NOT MENTIONED].
Physical exam: His heart and lungs sound good, no swelling in the ankles and feet, no pain or discomfort during the examination.
Test Results: Potassium level 4.0, last checked 1 week ago. [NO OTHER TEST RESULTS MENTIONED]
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: Mr. Roberts has resistant hypertension, with a blood pressure that is not responding to maximum dose of three medications.
Problem list: Hypertension; Resistant Hypertension; [NO OTHER PROBLEMS MENTIONED]
Plan:
I have added Spironolactone to his regimen to address the potential secondary cause of his hypertension, specifically hyperaldosterone.
I will monitor his potassium and sodium levels closely, and schedule a follow-up in 2 weeks for a basic metabolic panel and blood pressure check.
If the new medication does not work, the next step would be to refer him to the Resistant Hypertension Clinic for further evaluation and potential procedures, such as renal denervation.
Follow-up: We will see Mr. Roberts in 2 weeks to check his blood pressure and electrolytes.
